Unit.description: Contains generic information about the unit - description, dependencies, ordering.

Service.description: Configuration specific to service units - how to start, stop, and manage the process.

Install.description: Installation info used by systemctl enable/disable. Defines which target pulls in this unit.

Timer.description: Timer unit configuration. Modern replacement for cron jobs.

Type.description: Defines the process start-up type. simple: main process stays in foreground. forking: traditional daemon. oneshot: runs once.

ExecStart.description: The command to run when the service is started. Must be an absolute path.

Restart.description: Configures when the service should be automatically restarted.

RestartSec.description: Time to sleep before restarting a service. Default is 100ms.

User.description: Run the service as the specified user.

WorkingDirectory.description: Set the working directory for the executed process.

Environment.description: Set environment variables for the service process.

EnvironmentFile.description: Load environment variables from a file. Prefix with - to ignore if missing.

After.description: Defines ordering - start this unit after the listed units.

WantedBy.description: When enabled, this unit will be started by the specified target.

OnCalendar.description: Calendar event expression for timer units. Supports: daily, weekly, hourly, or specific timestamps.

LimitNOFILE.description: Set the max number of open file descriptors for the process.

ProtectSystem.description: Mount /usr and /boot as read-only. strict also mounts /etc as read-only.
